axes.name: interpreter continue-in-nested-if escape semantics
axes.summary: hexa interpreter mis-handles `continue` inside a nested `if` block: the `continue` exits the inner-if but execution continues with remaining statements of the outer-if before re-entering the loop. In _valid_grade the outer `if !suffix_started { ... return false } else { ... return false }` always hit a `return false` after the inner digit branch's `continue` for any valid grade.
axes.evidence: Trace fixture /tmp/test_grade2.hexa: input '7' -> i=0 c='7' digit_seen=false suffix_started=false -> 'no match -> false' (printed from outer-if's tail return, despite inner `if c >= '0' && c <= '9' { ...; continue }` matching). Fresh fixture /tmp/test_grade5.hexa with FLAT structure (no outer if-wrap) returned true correctly.

axes.name: flatten _valid_grade branches into single-level guards
axes.summary: Rewrote _valid_grade to use flat sequential `if ... { ...; continue }` guards keyed off the suffix_started boolean, eliminating all nested-if-with-continue paths. No call sites changed; same regex grammar. Comment also tightened: extended_token ::= [A-Z_][A-Z_0-9]*. Net delta -11 LoC (16-line body vs 27-line body).
axes.evidence: git diff: 1 hunk in fn _valid_grade only (lines ~287-317 of patched file). Diff: -21/+10 (excluding 3 added comment lines documenting the fix rationale). All other code paths untouched.

axes.name: 12-case selftest corpus including 9 valid + 3 invalid grades
axes.summary: Selftest corpus at lines 562-575 covers: numeric (7, 10), suffixed (10*, 11*), extended-token (10*PASS_LITERATURE, 10*PROBE_RUN, 10*BARRIER_CONFIRMED, 10*NEGATIVE_VERDICT, 11*REPO_INVARIANT), and rejection cases (empty, abc, 10*lower). Pre-fix: 9/12 fail. Post-fix: 12/12 pass.
axes.evidence_pre: [omega_cycle_atlas_ingest selftest] FAIL grade-validator: grade `7` → got false want true (and 8 more)
axes.evidence_post: [omega_cycle_atlas_ingest selftest] OK — type-classification 7 cases / grade-validator 12 cases / glob 46 witnesses / cross-repo nexus path / slug determinism / emit idempotence

axes.name: no downstream regression in --dry-run live mode
axes.summary: Beyond selftest, ran `--dry-run --repo-only nexus` end-to-end. Sentinel emitted: __OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ PASS cycles=46 facts=1161 crosslinks=0 repos=3 grade_dist=7:1013,10:0,10*ext:6,11:0,11*ext:142. Grade distribution non-zero across 7/10*ext/11*ext buckets confirms validator now accepts all live-classified grade tokens.

before: if c >= "0" && c <= "9" { digit_seen = true; i = i + 1; continue }
after: if suffix_started && ((c >= "A" && c <= "Z") || c == "_" || (c >= "0" && c <= "9")) { i = i + 1; continue }
if c >= "0" && c <= "9" { digit_seen = true; i = i + 1; continue }
if (c >= "A" && c <= "Z") || c == "_" { suffix_started = true; i = i + 1; continue }

selftest_output_post_fix: [omega_cycle_atlas_ingest selftest] OK — type-classification 7 cases / grade-validator 12 cases / glob 46 witnesses / cross-repo nexus path / slug determinism / emit idempotence
downstream_invariant: validator change is grammar-equivalent: same accept/reject set as the v2 author's intent; only the implementation control-flow pattern changed to avoid the interpreter quirk.
follow_ups: Same nested-if-continue pattern may exist elsewhere in nexus hexa tools — sweep candidates: tool/hexa_sim_atlas_ingest.hexa (already flat, OK), tool/atlas_dsl_v2_serializer.hexa::_valid_grade_v2 (TODO probe).
Consider filing a hexa-lang interpreter issue: `continue` inside nested `if` should re-enter loop test, not execute outer-if remaining statements.
method_gap: Author wrote idiomatic if/else block-structured code that would work in any other lang; interpreter fault, not author logic fault. Method gap = `selftest must run on the actual interpreter, not be code-reviewed mentally`. The bug only surfaced when `--selftest` was actually executed against the deployed hexa_interp.
